summary: First iteration full e2e testing of Aura Cosmetics. All 29 backend pytest cases pass (auth, products, news, orders, workers, max-4 enforcement, sales, follow_up_date=+30days, sales isolation, reminders, attendance clock-in/out, accounts with last_purchase, stats, notifications role filtering, products/news CRUD, questions, role-based 403 enforcement). Frontend home, products, login, director dashboard (overview with charts + recent orders/sales), and admin dashboard (products/news/questions tabs) all load and render real data after a one-time frontend restart to pick up updated REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL.

minor.issue: is_late is computed twice (lines 550-551 in server.py) — first assignment is dead code; harmless but confusing
CORS_ORIGINS="*" is set in env but unused (server hardcodes [FRONTEND_URL, localhost:3000]); remove to avoid confusion

integration_issues.issue: Frontend was running with a stale REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL pointing to https://beauty-flow-8.preview.emergentagent.com; restarting supervisor frontend picked up the correct URL from /app/frontend/.env and CORS errors disappeared. Note: dev-server requires restart on .env changes (hot reload does NOT pick up env vars).

action_items: On any future REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL change, remember to `sudo supervisorctl restart frontend` (CRA dev server caches env at process start).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is a single 934-line file — consider splitting into routers/auth/products/sales/etc. modules for maintainability.
/api/notifications role check is inside the handler instead of using require_roles(...) dependency (line 744) — inconsistent with rest of codebase but functionally correct.
Late detection uses raw `datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(hours=5)`; works but using a real timezone (zoneinfo 'Asia/Tashkent') would be clearer and DST-safe.
/api/orders does not validate quantity > 0 nor check product `in_stock`; trivial input validation gap.

Seed function runs on every startup and inserts only if collections empty — safe, but ADMIN_PASSWORD update path silently overwrites password if it changes; this is intended per playbook but worth documenting.

success_rate.frontend: ~95% (key flows verified; sub-tab clicks not exhaustively automated)

test_credentials: Admin: admin@aura.uz / Admin@2026 ; Director: director@aura.uz / Director@2026 ; Customer/Worker: created dynamically by tests
seed_data_creation: Tests register a fresh customer per session, create/cleanup test workers (test_worker_*, test_cap_*), produce one TEST_cust sale, one TEST_order, and one TEST_q question. Director account now has visible test data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend regression suite is at /app/backend/tests/test_aura_cosmetics.py — run with `pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v`. Frontend dev server picks up .env only after `supervisorctl restart frontend`. Director dashboard tabs (AKKAUNTLAR/SOTUVLAR/DAVOMAT/XARITA/ISHCHILAR) are rendered as buttons; using get_by_text exact=True with all-caps may time out due to CSS uppercase styling — prefer button[role='tab']-style selectors or data-testid. Adding data-testid to dashboard tab buttons would make UI testing more robust.
rca of the issue: Initial CORS errors were caused by the running frontend dev-server having been started before the current REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL value was written to /app/frontend/.env, so axios was hitting a different backend (beauty-flow-8...) which itself had CORS allow_origins=* (incompatible with credentials:include). Reproduction: change .env without restarting supervisor frontend. Mitigation applied: `sudo supervisorctl restart frontend`. After restart all API calls succeeded and CORS errors disappeared.
